narrative: Fire ecology features prominently this cycle, with a new trait-based framework for rapidly assessing fire impacts on data-poor Australian invertebrates and a climate-emulator tool (IAM-FIRE) projecting how emissions and development pathways will reshape global wildfire risk through 2100. Climate-driven range and behaviour shifts dominate elsewhere: reindeer grazing pressure is weakening as warming summers push animals to reduce activity in mountain tundra, mountain mammals and birds face elevated extinction risk as they track shifting climate envelopes into unfavourable land-use zones, and elephants alter both movement and habitat selection across drought timescales. Connectivity losses are stark in the Amazon, where four decades of deforestation have sharply reduced functional landscape connectivity for seed and pollen dispersal. On the demographic and community side, a nationwide study of over 92,000 individuals reveals spatially structured age-dependent survival in a long-lived species, while nitrogen fertilization is shown to promote wheat-weed coexistence through niche differentiation rather than competitive exclusion. Conservation highlights include associational defence among kelp-forest seaweeds limiting urchin grazing, with direct implications for restoration practice. Methodologically, deep-learning pipelines for animal activity recognition and machine/deep-learning approaches for transferring joint species distribution models both push toward more standardized, transferable ecological modelling tools. In evolution, polygenic adaptation in heavily fished Malawi cichlids and rainfall-driven adaptation with gene flow in a widespread butterfly underscore how contemporary selection pressures leave detectable genomic signatures.

articles.title: A trait‐based rapid assessment framework to estimate fire impacts on data‐poor Australian invertebrate taxa
articles.digest: New trait-based framework rapidly estimates fire impacts on Australia's poorly described invertebrate taxa, which make up over 90% of faunal diversity.

methods_radar.title: ‘SiteTool': a ‘Shiny' application for field site selection and evaluation
methods_radar.note: SiteTool: new R Shiny app for evidence-based field-site selection using remote-sensing data.

methods_radar.title: Socioeconomic traces of land use intensification in Uruguay: census meets remote sensing data
methods_radar.note: Combines census and remote-sensing data to trace land-use intensification in Uruguay.

methods_radar.title: Revealing the competing mechanisms between land use development and restoration in wetland evolution: an interpretable deep learning approach
methods_radar.note: Interpretable deep-learning approach disentangles land-use vs restoration drivers of wetland change.

methods_radar.title: RAPID: Real‐time animal pattern re‐identification on edge devices, an open‐source tool for field deployment
methods_radar.note: RAPID: open-source real-time animal re-identification tool for low-power edge devices like camera traps.

methods_radar.title: Harnessing edge computing and citizen science: A new prototype design for continental‐scale acoustic monitoring in Australia and beyond
methods_radar.note: New open-source modular acoustic-monitoring platform combining edge computing and citizen science, piloted in Australia.

methods_radar.title: A continuous‐time random encounter and staying time (REST) model: Moving beyond temporal aggregation in camera‐trap density estimation
methods_radar.note: New continuous-time REST model improves camera-trap density estimation without a synchronized-activity assumption.

methods_radar.title: pylifemap: Mapping data onto the tree of life
methods_radar.note: pylifemap: new Python package for visualizing data on the interactive tree of life.

methods_radar.title: AI‐based identification of Chinese vascular plants from herbarium specimens: a tool for all herbaria with Chinese holdings
methods_radar.note: New AI model identifies Chinese vascular plants from herbarium images across over 21,000 species.

methods_radar.title: Satellite hindcasts of foliar traits reveal a subtle but consistent relaxation of conservativeness in a biodiverse mountain grassland over the last four decades
methods_radar.note: Combines field trait data with satellite hindcasts to track four decades of functional change in mountain grassland.

methods_radar.title: Harnessing the power of machine and deep learning for transferring joint species distribution models considering the structure of biotic interactions
methods_radar.note: Benchmarks machine- and deep-learning approaches for improving transferability of joint species distribution models.

methods_radar.title: The effect of trait choice on hybrid species distribution model projections under climate change
methods_radar.note: Evaluates how trait choice affects hybrid species distribution model projections under climate change.

methods_radar.title: Estimation of Forest Soil Organic Carbon Density in Dry Tropical Valleys Based on Multi-source Data and Machine Learning Algorithm
methods_radar.note: Applies multi-source data and machine learning to estimate forest soil organic carbon density.

methods_radar.title: Long-Term Vegetation Changes Across the Canadian Forest-Tundra Ecotone Using Historic Air Photos and Satellite Time-Series
methods_radar.note: Combines historic air photos with satellite time series to track decades of forest-tundra vegetation change.

methods_radar.title: Satellite Remote Sensing of Alpine Vegetation Dynamics: Challenges and Perspectives
methods_radar.note: Perspective on pitfalls interpreting satellite greening trends in alpine vegetation monitoring.

methods_radar.title: Artificial intelligence and decision support in applied ecology
methods_radar.note: Proposes a roadmap for evaluating and applying AI tools responsibly in applied-ecology decisions.

methods_radar.title: Optimal deep learning activity recognition pipeline using animal bio‐logging data
methods_radar.note: Benchmarks and standardizes deep-learning pipelines for animal activity recognition from bio-logging sensors.

methods_radar.title: Filtration of airborne eDNA outperforms leaf swabs for terrestrial vertebrate detection in a temperate mixed forest with longer filtration times performing the best
methods_radar.note: Benchmarks airborne eDNA filtration against leaf swabs for detecting forest vertebrates.

methods_radar.title: Integrated analysis of fossils and molecular divergence time estimates a latest Jurassic origin of angiosperms
methods_radar.note: Combines fossil data with Bayesian molecular-clock calibration to date angiosperm origin to the Late Jurassic.

methods_radar.title: Bridging data silos to holistically model plant macrophenology
methods_radar.note: Proposes a framework to harmonize herbaria, citizen-science, and remote-sensing data for plant macrophenology.

methods_radar.title: On herbarium specimen images and artificial intelligence
methods_radar.note: Perspective on best practices and pitfalls of using AI models trained on herbarium specimen images.

methods_radar.title: Next‐generation specimen digitization: capturing reflectance spectra from the world's herbaria for modeling plant biology across time, space, and taxa
methods_radar.note: Calls for a coordinated global database of herbarium spectral-reflectance data for cross-taxa trait modeling.

methods_radar.title: Changing patterns of colonisation and persistence during the wolf recolonisation of the human‐dominated Italian alpine region
methods_radar.note: Uses dynamic occupancy models to track wolf recolonisation patterns in the Italian Alps.

methods_radar.title: Spectrally Guided Delineation of Structurally Homogeneous Vegetation Patches Using Sentinel-2 Imagery in Heterogeneous Post-Industrial Landscapes
methods_radar.note: Uses Sentinel-2 imagery to delineate vegetation patches in post-industrial landscapes.

methods_radar.title: eDNA signals improve local predictions of fish abundances in Mediterranean coastal ecosystems
methods_radar.note: Uses eDNA metabarcoding to improve fish abundance predictions in coastal species distribution models.

methods_radar.title: Understanding variability and complexity of airborne fungal eDNA
methods_radar.note: Reviews variability and complexity challenges in airborne fungal eDNA monitoring methods.

methods_radar.title: A fungal perspective: Ecological reinterpretation of short‐term temporal variation in airborne eDNA
methods_radar.note: Reinterprets short-term airborne eDNA variation for fungal community monitoring.

methods_radar.title: DNA Metabarcoding Reveals Unexpected Predator–Prey–Microbial Dynamics in the Southern Right Whale (Eubalaena australis)
methods_radar.note: Uses DNA metabarcoding to reveal diet and gut-microbiome links in recovering southern right whale populations.

methods_radar.title: Environmental temperature shapes heart size evolution in birds
methods_radar.note: Uses Bayesian phylogenetic mixed models to test Hesse's rule linking heart size to temperature across 915 bird species.
